title: 1. Introduction to DevOps
summary: What DevOps is, why it matters, and the culture behind it.
notes: DevOps = Development + Operations: a culture and set of practices that unify software development and IT operations.
Goals: faster delivery, higher quality, better collaboration, automation everywhere.
Pillars: Culture, Automation, Lean, Measurement, Sharing (CALMS).
Key metrics: Lead time, Deployment frequency, MTTR, Change failure rate (DORA metrics).

title: 2. Linux Fundamentals
summary: Shell, file system, permissions, processes, networking basics.
notes: Common commands: ls, cd, cp, mv, rm, grep, find, awk, sed, tar, ps, top, systemctl.
Permissions: chmod / chown, octal notation (rwx = 4+2+1).
Networking: ip a, ss, netstat, ping, curl, dig, traceroute.
Package managers: apt (Debian/Ubuntu), yum/dnf (RHEL/Fedora).

title: 3. Shell & Python Scripting
summary: Automate everything with Bash and Python.
notes: Bash: variables, loops, conditionals, functions, exit codes, set -euo pipefail.
Python: requests, subprocess, os, pathlib, argparse, logging.
Idempotency: scripts should be safe to run multiple times.

title: 4. Git & Version Control
summary: Branches, merges, pull requests, GitFlow vs trunk-based.
notes: Core: init, clone, add, commit, push, pull, branch, merge, rebase, stash, log.
Workflows: GitFlow, GitHub Flow, Trunk-based development.
Pull request reviews, conventional commits, semantic versioning.

title: 5. CI/CD Pipelines
summary: Continuous Integration and Delivery with Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI.
notes: CI: every commit triggers build + tests automatically.
CD: every passing build is deployable (Continuous Delivery) or auto-deployed (Continuous Deployment).
Jenkinsfile (declarative pipeline) vs GitHub Actions (YAML) vs GitLab CI (.gitlab-ci.yml).
Stages: checkout → build → test → security scan → package → deploy.

title: 6. Docker & Containers
summary: Build, ship, run anywhere with containers.
notes: Image vs container: image = blueprint, container = running instance.
Dockerfile basics: FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, RUN, EXPOSE, CMD, ENTRYPOINT.
docker compose up for multi-container apps (db + app + cache).
Best practices: multi-stage builds, small base images (alpine/distroless), .dockerignore.

title: 7. Kubernetes
notes: Core objects: Pod, Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Secret, Ingress, Namespace.
kubectl get/describe/apply/logs/exec — your daily commands.
Helm = package manager for Kubernetes.
Probes: liveness, readiness, startup.

title: 8. Infrastructure as Code (Terraform & Ansible)
summary: Declarative infrastructure with Terraform; configuration management with Ansible.
notes: Terraform: providers, resources, state, modules, plan/apply/destroy.
Ansible: playbooks (YAML), inventory, roles, idempotent tasks (no agent needed).
Store state remotely (S3 + DynamoDB lock) for team work.

title: 9. Monitoring & Observability
summary: Metrics, logs, traces — Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Loki.
notes: Three pillars: Metrics (Prometheus), Logs (Loki/ELK), Traces (Jaeger/Tempo).
Visualize with Grafana dashboards; alert with Alertmanager.
Define SLI / SLO / SLA. Track error budgets.

title: 10. Cloud Platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP)
summary: Core compute, storage, networking, IAM across the big three.
notes: AWS: EC2, S3, IAM, VPC, RDS, EKS, Lambda, CloudWatch.
Azure: VMs, Blob, AAD, AKS, Functions, Monitor.
GCP: Compute Engine, Cloud Storage, IAM, GKE, Cloud Functions, Stackdriver.

title: 11. DevSecOps
summary: Shift security left: SAST, DAST, SCA, secret scanning.
notes: SAST: Static code analysis (SonarQube, Semgrep).
DAST: Runtime testing (OWASP ZAP).
SCA: Dependency vulnerability scanning (Snyk, Trivy).
Secrets in Vault / AWS Secrets Manager — never in git.

title: 12. Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)
summary: Google's discipline: reliability as a feature.
notes: Toil reduction through automation (target < 50% toil).
Blameless postmortems after incidents.
Chaos engineering (Chaos Monkey, Gremlin) to test resilience.
On-call rotations and runbooks.
